free_text: Neutron inelastic neutron scattering cross sections
from 159Tb(n,n'gamma)
159Tb disk scatterer.
Pulsed proton beam on metallic lithium target.
Time-of-flight used to reduce unwanted events not
coincident with prompt gammas from inelastic
scattering.
Well-shielded coaxial germanium detector
surrounded by a NaI(Tl)annulus.
Plastic scintillator used to normalize neutron
scattering runs with sample in to incident neutron
fluence runs with sample out.
Neutron fluence incident upon the scatterer
was determined using a 235U-fission chamber with an
associated TOF system.
235U fission chamber.
Gamma yields corrected using codes NEVES and PENHA-b
for:
. neutron multiple scattering,
. neutron and gamma-ray attenuation.
. contribution from second neutron group from p-Li7
source.
No information on source of uncertainties.
Data received by email from Pil-Neyo Seo,
27 September 2001.
No reply from Pil-Neyo Seo to author proof.
DR NSTS reference added
SD: Ref. 2001TSUKUB,(361) deleted (article
published in NSTS,2,(1),397).
. Calculated from 125 degree data by summing
corrected gamma-ray production cross sections for
transitions originating from a level and subtracting
those for feeding transitions from higher levels.
Corrected for internal conversion.
